ja-gram-n3-1565-fill-2: ようとする shows an attempted action that failed before completion. Here it marks the speaker's failed kiss attempt when mom entered. Unlike おうとすると (momentary interruption) or ものだから (excuse), this emphasizes the unsuccessful effort itself.
ja-gram-n3-1671-fill-0: おうとすると means 'just when trying to do' — it captures the exact moment an action was interrupted. In 'I was about to get off the bus when I got pushed,' it shows the action (getting off) was stopped mid-attempt. Differs from ようとする (unsuccessful attempt) by emphasizing the point of interruption.
ja-gram-n3-1671-fill-1: おうとすると marks the precise moment before a planned action occurs, suddenly interrupted. 'Just about to go out when dad called' — the action was stopped right at the threshold. More specific than ながら (while doing) or ようとする (try to do).
ja-gram-n3-1671-fill-3: おうとすると captures the critical moment when you're about to act, then get interrupted. 'When trying to propose, a friend suddenly came' — the timing is right before the action starts. Distinct from ようとする by emphasizing the interruption event, not the attempt itself.
ja-gram-n3-609-fill-0: て connects sequential or parallel actions with a flow. 'Bringing the bowl... and shoveling rice' uses て to link two related movements in one clause. Unlike し (multiple reasons), か何か (or something), or と共に (simultaneous), て is the natural connector for describing verb chains.
ja-gram-n3-609-fill-1: て links adjectives and nouns describing qualities together. 'Red and large car' — て joins 赤くて (red) to 大きい (large) to pile up describing words. This is the standard adjective-connector role, not し (reasons) or か何か (vagueness).
ja-gram-n3-609-fill-2: て sequences two actions naturally: 'go to bookstore, (then) buy.' It's the basic verb-connector showing consecutive action flow. More direct than と共に (together), し (and/because), or か何か (or something).
ja-gram-n3-609-fill-3: て chains actions in sequence: 'ate melon, drank coffee' — showing two consecutive events in past. Unlike し (multiple reasons) or と共に (simultaneous), て shows simple chronological order without emphasizing cause or simultaneity.
ja-gram-n3-609-fill-4: て connects a method with a result: 'come on foot (walking)' — walking is HOW you come. Standard te-form connector for describing the means/manner of an action. Not か何か (vague) or と共に (simultaneous).
ja-gram-n3-1707-fill-0: か何か signals vagueness or 'something like that' — 'buy you a coke or something' relaxes specificity. Unlike て (action connector), し (multiple reasons), or であろうが (regardless of choice), it expresses casual uncertainty about the exact item.
ja-gram-n3-1707-fill-2: か何か shows 'or something' uncertainty — 'there are monkeys or something there' softens the claim. Different from て (connector), と共に (with), or し (multiple reasons) because it means you're unsure of exactness, not just listing items.
ja-gram-n3-1707-fill-3: か何か means 'or something' — used when you're not specific about what you want. 'Give me a cool drink or something' relaxes the request. Not て (sequential), し (multiple reasons), or と共に (simultaneous).
ja-gram-n3-1707-fill-4: か何か softens a desire with vagueness: 'want to play baseball or something' — you're unsure or casual about it. Unlike て (connector), し (reasons), or であろうが (regardless), it emphasizes uncertainty over specifics.
ja-gram-n3-1781-fill-0: であろうが means 'whether A or B, it doesn't matter' — regardless of which option. 'Mini or Air iPad, doesn't matter, I want it' — both choices lead to same result. Differs from て (sequence), し (reasons), or か何か (vague) by showing indifference to choice.
ja-gram-n3-1781-fill-1: であろうが expresses 'regardless of either option' — 'whether nice-to-live or not-nice-to-live, cheap rent matters.' Both scenarios (good or bad) are equally overridden by another priority. Not て (connector), し (and), or というのに (despite).
ja-gram-n3-733-fill-0: である is the formal copula 'to be' — used in written/formal speech. 'The doctor Tanaka' where である marks the identity. Unlike なる (become), ばかり (just did), or もとい (rather), である is the standard formal 'is/are' verb.
ja-gram-n3-733-fill-1: である is formal 'to be' in conditionals: 'Even if that is [correct]...' structures formal reasoning. Unlike もとい (correction), なぜなら (because), or な (adjective particle), である provides the formal copula link needed here.
ja-gram-n3-733-fill-2: である makes formal identity statements: 'To always be honest is not easy' — である forms the noun phrase 'to be always honest.' This formal link differs from ばかり (just did), な (adjective), or なる (become).
ja-gram-n3-638-fill-0: な is the na-adjective particle connecting adjective to noun: 'strange person' — 変 becomes 変な (strange) with な. Unlike である (formal), なぜなら (because), or わざとらしい (fake), な is the required marker for na-adjectives modifying nouns.
ja-gram-n3-638-fill-1: な marks a na-adjective: 'beautiful legs' — きれい becomes きれいな with な to modify 足. Standard na-adjective particle role. Not ばかり (just), もとい (rather), である (is), or なぜなら (because).
ja-gram-n3-638-fill-2: な connects na-adjective to noun: 'quiet restaurant' — 静か becomes 静かな with な. The required na-adjective modifier. Unlike なる (become), なぜなら (reason), もとい (correction), or ばかり (just did).
ja-gram-n3-1657-fill-0: わざとらしい means 'fake, artificial' — describes false/pretend behavior. 'An artificial smile' describes forced, fake emotion. Unlike である (is), な (adjective particle), もとい (rather), or なる (become), わざとらしい emphasizes deception.
ja-gram-n3-1657-fill-1: わざとらしい describes 'affected, fake' manner: 'his fake/affected way of speaking' — an unnatural, put-on style. Unlike なる (become), なぜなら (reason), である (is), or な (particle), it means deliberately fake.
ja-gram-n3-1657-fill-2: わざとらしい means 'fake, pretending' — 'his injury looks fake/put-on.' Unlike である (is), なる (become), な (particle), or もとい (rather), it shows something is deliberately false or exaggerated.
ja-gram-n3-640-fill-0: なる can function like な for na-adjectives in archaic/poetic style: '聖なる剣' (sacred sword) uses なる as a na-adjective marker. Here it's interchangeable with な in formal/literary contexts. Different from もとい (rather), わざとらしい (fake), or である (is).
ja-gram-n3-640-fill-1: なる works like な for na-adjectives in formal/poetic language: '新たなる希望' (a new hope) — なる marks the adjective in elegant style. Unlike な (casual), である (is), わざとらしい (fake), or なぜなら (because).
ja-gram-n3-1587-fill-0: もとい means 'rather, I mean' — a correction marker. 'Date... rather, offline-meeting...' shows you're revising what you just said. Unlike な (adjective), なる (become), である (is), or わざとらしい (fake), もとい undoes a statement.
ja-gram-n3-974-fill-0: によると means 'according to' — reports attribution from a source. 'According to his good education' attributes success cause to education. Unlike とのこと (hearsay), と言うと (speaking of), 旨 (that/concerning), や と同様 (same way), it formally cites an informant.
ja-gram-n3-974-fill-1: によると indicates 'according to' what someone said: 'According to my friend's story, the movie is interesting' — citing the friend as source. Different from と言うと (when you mention), とのこと (reported), 旨 (concerning), or 並みに (to degree).
ja-gram-n3-974-fill-2: によると formally attributes a claim to sources: 'According to NBC News and Reuters, Bush plans...' — multiple sources cited. Not とのこと (reported speech), 並みに (to same degree), 旨 (that), or と言うと (speaking of).
ja-gram-n3-974-fill-3: によると indicates 'credited/attributed to' — 'The light bulb's invention is credited to Edison' — showing who gets credit. Different from 旨 (that), 並みに (same degree), と言うと (when mention), or とのこと (reported).
ja-gram-n3-974-fill-4: によると means 'according to' an estimate: 'According to his estimate, new house costs 30 million yen.' Unlike とのこと (reported), と言うと (speaking of), 並みに (to same degree), or 旨 (that), it cites a specific source.
ja-gram-n3-1145-fill-0: と言うと means 'when you mention/speak of' — triggers association. 'When you say Switzerland, what comes to mind?' — the name prompts a response. Not によると (according to), 並みに (to same degree), や (and), or ってば (insistently).
ja-gram-n3-1145-fill-1: と言うと introduces a clarification: 'To say why he wasn't fired...' — it frames the explanation that follows. Unlike とのこと (reported), によると (according to), や (and), or 旨 (that), it sets up the reason.
ja-gram-n3-1145-fill-2: と言うと means 'whatever/whenever' — 'Whatever happens, he always butts in.' Shows conditional pattern. Not によると (according to), 旨 (that), とのこと (reported), or ってば (insistently).
ja-gram-n3-1669-fill-0: 旨 means 'that/concerning' — reports gist of words: 'The prosecutor's words THAT the prosecution would be delayed...' — summarizes the core message. Unlike とのこと (reported), 並みに (to degree), によると (according), or と言うと (speaking of).
ja-gram-n3-1710-fill-0: ～たっけ means 'was it/did I recall correctly?' — seeks confirmation of past fact. 'Was it that good?' checks if memory is right. Unlike よくも～ものだ (how dare), うちに (while), なかったろう (probably not), or のに (I wish), it's about recalling.
ja-gram-n3-1710-fill-1: ～たっけ asks to confirm what you did: 'When did we go see a movie?' retrieves shared memory. Not なかったろう (probably didn't), のに (wish), よくも～ものだ (how dare), or うちに (while before changes).
ja-gram-n3-1710-fill-2: ～たっけ asks where you left off: 'Where did I stop talking?' seeks to recall. Different from よくも～ものだ (how dare), うちに (during transition), のに (wish), or なかったろう (probably not).
ja-gram-n3-1710-fill-3: ～たっけ seeks to recall: 'Where is John's house again?' asks for memory refresh. Not のに (wish), うちに (before change), なかったろう (probably not), or よくも～ものだ (how dare).
ja-gram-n3-1710-fill-4: ～たっけ retrieves memory: 'What did I eat for lunch today?' checks recall. Unlike うちに (while), なかったろう (probably not), よくも～ものだ (how dare), or のに (wish), it's memory-seeking.
ja-gram-n3-1787-fill-0: よくも～ものだ expresses indignation: 'How the hell could he eat a whole cake by himself?' shows disbelief/anger. Unlike なかったろう (probably not), うちに (during), たっけ (recall), or のに (wish), it's emphatic blame.
ja-gram-n3-779-fill-0: べき means 'should' based on moral/rational expectation: 'I should have come earlier' — reflects what was right to do. Unlike てはいけない (must not), なければならない (must), 予定 (planned), or 方が (better), べき is about moral should.
ja-gram-n3-779-fill-1: べき means 'should do' — 'We should read many books' expresses proper/expected behavior. Not 積もりだった (intended), なければなりません (must), なさい (do!), or 方が (better).
ja-gram-n3-779-fill-2: べき means 'should not' in negative: 'You shouldn't rely on others' — what's improper. Different from 方が (better), てはいけない (forbidden), てもいいですか (allowed?), or 予定 (planned).
ja-gram-n3-779-fill-3: べき asks the philosophical question: 'How should one live?' — the right way to live. Not 筈 (should be true), 方が (better), せねばならない (must), or 予定 (planned).
ja-gram-n3-779-fill-4: べき means 'should' in indirect questions: 'when I should leave' — the appropriate time. Unlike てはいけない (must not), 予定 (planned), なければなりません (must), or 筈 (supposed).
ja-gram-n3-757-fill-0: たい means 'wanting to do' — expresses desire. 'I wanna see the Bond movie!' shows direct want. Not せねばならない (must), 予定 (planned), なければならない (must), or 筈 (should be).
ja-gram-n3-757-fill-1: たい expresses desire: 'I want to buy this car but I have no money' — shows what you wish for. Unlike なければなりません (must), なければならない (must), てはいけない (must not), or 筈 (expected).
ja-gram-n3-757-fill-2: たい asks about wants: 'What would you like to do?' probes desire. Not なければなりません (must), 予定 (planned), 筈 (expected), or べき (should).
ja-gram-n3-1772-fill-0: なければなりません is formal 'must' — obligation stated politely. 'You must eat vegetables' — a firm requirement in polite form. Unlike てはいけない (forbidden), べき (should), なければならない (casual must), or 積もりだった (intended).
ja-gram-n3-1772-fill-1: なければなりません means 'must' in formal context: 'I must buy eggs at the store' — formal necessity. Not 積もりだった (intended), せねばならない (formal must), 筈 (expected), or 予定 (planned).
ja-gram-n3-1772-fill-2: なければなりません expresses obligation: 'I must study Japanese daily' — daily requirement in formal register. Unlike なければならない (casual), てはいけない (forbidden), 予定 (planned), or べき (should).
ja-gram-n3-1154-fill-3: 筈 means 'is supposed to be/expected' — what should logically be true. 'That cannot be' (そんな筈はない) denies an expectation. Not なければならない (must), せねばならない (must), みたい (seems), or 予定 (planned).
ja-gram-n3-1661-fill-0: てはいけません means 'must not' — prohibition. 'One must not harass animals' forbids the action. Unlike せねばならない (must), な (particle), なければならない (must), or 予定 (planned), it's absolute prohibition.
ja-gram-n3-1661-fill-1: てはいけません forbids an action: 'You cannot smoke here because it's no-smoking' — strict rule. Different from 筈 (expected), 積もりだった (intended), なさい (do!), or せねばならない (must).
ja-gram-n3-1661-fill-3: てはいけない forbids behavior: 'It is forbidden to park bicycles here' — legal/rule-based prohibition. Not 予定 (planned), 筈 (expected), なければならない (must), or なさい (do!).
ja-gram-n3-1661-fill-4: てはいけない forbids an action: 'I told him not to eat salty food' — the prohibition was stated. Unlike なければなりません (must), 予定 (planned), 筈 (expected), or な (particle).
ja-gram-n3-1709-fill-0: なければならない means casual 'must' — obligation without formality. 'I must do my homework' — straightforward necessity. Unlike なければなりません (formal), 予定 (planned), てはいけない (forbidden), or べき (should).
ja-gram-n3-1709-fill-1: なければならない is casual 'must': 'I have to study Japanese' — everyday obligation. Different from せねばならない (formal), 筈 (expected), べき (should), or なければなりません (polite formal).
ja-gram-n3-1709-fill-2: なければならない means 'must' — 'I have to take my finals this week' — requirement. Not てはいけない (forbidden), べき (should), 積もりだった (intended), or 予定 (planned).
ja-gram-n3-1709-fill-3: なければならない expresses necessity: 'I must eat breakfast' — required action. Unlike べき (should), せねばならない (formal), 筈 (expected), or 予定 (planned).
ja-gram-n3-1709-fill-4: なければならない means 'must': 'I must leave around 10' — time-based necessity. Not 積もりだった (intended), てはいけない (forbidden), なければなりません (formal), or べき (should).
ja-gram-n3-1519-fill-0: せねばならない is formal/literary 'must' — formal necessity. 'Those involved must tell the truth' — formal obligation. Unlike としても (even if), 予定 (planned), てはいけない (forbidden), or 積もりだった (intended).
ja-gram-n3-1519-fill-1: せねばならない is formal 'must': 'We must develop new markets for these products' — formal duty. Different from と (and), なければなりません (polite), 予定 (planned), or としても (even if).
ja-gram-n3-1519-fill-2: せねばならない means formal 'must': 'I must achieve my goal at all costs' — stern duty. Not なければならない (casual), てはいけない (forbidden), としても (even if), or 筈 (expected).
ja-gram-n3-1519-fill-3: せねばならない expresses formal obligation: 'You must respect the elderly' — formal duty. Unlike と (and), 積もりだった (intended), なければならない (casual), or としても (even if).
ja-gram-n3-1519-fill-4: せねばならない is formal 'must': 'I must study hard to keep up with him' — formal commitment. Not 予定 (planned), なければなりません (polite), てはいけない (forbidden), or べき (should).
ja-gram-n3-1302-fill-1: させる is causative 'make/let someone do' — you cause another to act. 'Make your son independent' — you're the agent causing independence. Different from っぽい (seems), 出す (put out), やすい (easy), or 方 (method/way).
ja-gram-n3-1302-fill-2: させる means causative 'make/let' — 'You should make your son leave home' — causing another's action. Not 方 (method), 直す (fix), やすい (easy), or 切る (cut).
ja-gram-n3-1302-fill-3: させる is causative: 'I don't mean to make you worry' — you cause the other's emotional state. Unlike っぽい (seems), 合う (suit), にくい (hard), or 直す (fix).
ja-gram-n3-1302-fill-4: させる means 'make someone do' — 'I could never make him believe what I said' — compelling belief in another. Not 合う (suit), 方 (way), がち (tend to), or 出す (put).
ja-gram-n3-1734-fill-0: ということですね rephrases for confirmation: 'So it's a gradual thing, isn't it?' — checks your understanding. Unlike 下さいませんか (please), どうぞ (go ahead), と伝えていただけませんか (convey), or であろう (maybe).
ja-gram-n3-1720-fill-0: 下さいませんか is polite request: 'Would you give me something to drink?' — courteous asking. Not ということですね (confirm), と伝えていただけませんか (convey), どうぞ (go ahead), or を聞いた (asked about).
ja-gram-n3-1720-fill-1: 下さいませんか asks politely: 'Could you introduce yourself in English?' — formal request. Different from ということですね (confirm), どうぞ (go ahead), と伝えていただけませんか (convey), or の様 (like).
ja-gram-n3-1720-fill-2: 下さいませんか requests politely: 'Could you lend me the book?' — courteous asking. Not どうぞ (go ahead), と伝えていただけませんか (convey), ということですね (confirm), or 気がする (feels like).
ja-gram-n3-1720-fill-3: 下さいませんか politely requests: 'Could you give me seconds?' — formal asking. Unlike と伝えていただけませんか (convey), ということですね (confirm), どうぞ (go ahead), or なのに (despite).
ja-gram-n3-1720-fill-4: 下さいませんか asks formally: 'Would you explain that more clearly?' — polite request. Not どうぞ (go ahead), ということですね (confirm), と伝えていただけませんか (convey), or ように言う (tell-as).
ja-gram-n3-1717-fill-0: どうぞ means 'please go ahead' — permissive invitation. 'Please sit down' offers the seat. Not と伝えていただけませんか (convey), ということですね (confirm), 下さいませんか (please), or あまり (not very).
ja-gram-n3-1717-fill-1: どうぞ invites freely: 'Help yourself to snacks' — encourages taking. Unlike 下さいませんか (please give), と伝えていただけませんか (convey), ということですね (confirm), or によると (according).
ja-gram-n3-1717-fill-2: どうぞ allows staying: 'Please keep your seats' — gives permission to stay. Not ということですね (confirm), 下さいませんか (please), と伝えていただけませんか (convey), or ところで (by the way).
ja-gram-n3-1717-fill-3: どうぞ invites relaxation: 'Take your time here' — encourages comfort. Different from ということですね (confirm), と伝えていただけませんか (convey), 下さいませんか (please), or 次第に (gradually).
ja-gram-n3-1717-fill-4: どうぞ gives permission to speak: 'Go ahead and say it' — invites expression. Not と伝えていただけませんか (convey), 下さいませんか (please), ということですね (confirm), or Q~でも (or even).
ja-gram-n3-1598-fill-0: と伝えていただけませんか asks someone to relay your message: 'Please tell him congratulations' — requesting message delivery. Unlike 下さいませんか (please), どうぞ (go ahead), ということですね (confirm), or なくてもすむ (can skip).
ja-gram-n3-1130-fill-1: ために means 'in order to' (purpose) or 'because of' (cause): 'I called to confirm he was coming' — the phone call's purpose. Different from なぜなら (because), ものだから (excuse), ようになる (change), or おかげで (thanks to).
ja-gram-n3-1130-fill-2: ために means 'because of' (cause): 'He died last year of old age' — cause of death. Not ことから (from fact), なぜなら (reason), せいで (fault), or おかげで (thanks to).
ja-gram-n3-1130-fill-3: ために expresses purpose: 'What's this key for?' — the key's intended use. Different from ことにする (decide), ように (goal), 意向形と思う (I think), or 予定 (planned).
ja-gram-n3-1130-fill-4: ために shows purpose: 'There are no good dictionaries for foreigners' — who they're meant for. Not せいで (fault), それで (so), 予定 (planned), or ことから (from).
ja-gram-n3-1504-fill-0: ようになる shows changed ability: 'He lived in Japan but never learned to speak Japanese!' — he didn't become able. Unlike 積もりだった (intended), 予定 (planned), ことにする (decide), or ために (purpose).
ja-gram-n3-1504-fill-1: ようになる means 'has reached the point of doing' — changed capacity. 'My child has started cleaning her room herself' — gained ability/habit. Different from 意向形と思う (I'll), ことにする (decide), にする (make), or 積もりだった (intended).
ja-gram-n3-1504-fill-2: ようになる shows future change: 'The baby will soon start smiling' — upcoming development. Not 次第に (gradually), ことにする (decide), 予定 (planned), or ことになる (decided by others).
ja-gram-n3-1504-fill-3: ようになる indicates habit change: 'Since I came to Japan, I got used to eating tofu' — acquired new habit. Unlike ことにする (decide), 予定 (planned), ために (purpose), or にする (make).
ja-gram-n3-1504-fill-4: ようになる shows ability change: 'Mr. Renz had reached the point where he could speak Japanese' — achieved language skill. Not ように (manner), 次第に (gradually), ことにする (decide), or ことになる (decided).
ja-gram-n3-1482-fill-0: 予定 means 'it's a plan' — something scheduled but not yet done. 'This is only a plan and nothing more' — emphasizes it's not yet reality. Different from ことにする (decide), なければなりません (must), ために (purpose), or 筈 (supposed).
ja-gram-n3-1482-fill-1: 予定 means 'planning to' — 'I plan to finish this tomorrow' — intended schedule. Not 意向形と思う (I'll), ことにする (decide), ように (goal), or べき (should).
ja-gram-n3-1482-fill-2: 予定 shows intention: 'I plan to go home next week' — scheduled departure. Unlike ようになる (change), ことにする (decide), 積もりだった (intended), or ように (goal).
ja-gram-n3-1482-fill-3: 予定 means 'planning to': 'Next year I'm planning to marry her' — future marriage plan. Not なければなりません (must), べき (should), ことにする (decide), or ように (goal).
ja-gram-n3-1629-fill-0: ことにする is active decision: 'I decided not to eat hamburgers daily' — you chose this path. Unlike ために (purpose), ように (goal), ことにする (already decided), or 意向形と思う (I'll).
ja-gram-n3-1629-fill-1: ことにする shows a made decision: 'To pass the exam, I decided to study harder' — volitional commitment. Not ように (goal state), ことにする (already decided), になる (becomes), or 積もりだった (intended).
ja-gram-n3-1632-fill-0: 積もりだった shows unfulfilled intent: 'I intended to be a doctor' — something you planned but didn't do. Unlike ことにする (decide), てはいけない (forbidden), ために (purpose), or ようになる (changed).
ja-gram-n3-1632-fill-1: 積もりだった expresses canceled intent: 'This was meant as a joke' — what you intended, not reality. Different from せねばならない (must), てはいけない (forbidden), 筈 (expected), or 意向形と思う (I'll).
ja-gram-n3-1632-fill-2: 積もりだった shows uncompleted plan: 'I was planning on going to Hawaii this summer' — intended but didn't happen. Not 筈 (expected), なければならない (must), ように (goal), or てはいけない (forbidden).
ja-gram-n3-1632-fill-3: 積もりだった expresses unrealized intent: 'I had intended to marry her' — planned but never happened. Not 予定 (planned currently), せねばならない (must), てはいけない (forbidden), or 筈 (expected).
ja-gram-n3-1632-fill-4: 積もりだった means 'I hadn't intended' — what you planned not to do. 'Nobody should come and I hadn't intended to leave either.' Not 予定 (current plan), ようになる (change), 筈 (expected), or べき (should).
ja-gram-n3-1268-fill-0: ように means 'in order to achieve' — goal-oriented use: 'Use the example above in order to [understand]' — the example serves your learning purpose. Not ことにする (decide), ようになる (changed), ために (purpose/cause), or 予定 (planned).
ja-gram-n3-1268-fill-1: ように shows prevention goal: 'In order not to leave things, take care' — the instruction's purpose. Different from 意向形と思う (I'll), ことにする (decide), 積もりだった (intended), or 予定 (planned).
ja-gram-n3-1268-fill-2: ように asks for action with a goal: 'Please delete [if you have the data]' — requesting action toward a state. Not 意向形と思う (I'll), のような (like), ことにする (decide), or ために (purpose/cause).
ja-gram-n3-1268-fill-3: ように expresses a wish/prayer: 'I pray you get better soon' — desired outcome. Not ために (purpose), ことにする (decide), よう (vague future), or みたい (seems).
ja-gram-n3-1268-fill-4: ように means 'so that' — goal-achieving change: 'To make management easier, I'll change gradually' — improving toward a state. Not ことにする (decide), よう (vague), 予定 (planned), or みたい (seems).
ja-gram-n3-1769-fill-0: ことにする shows deliberate decision: 'I have decided to quit smoking' — active choice made. Different from ために (purpose), ことにする (already decided), にする (makes), or 予定 (planned).
ja-gram-n3-737-fill-0: の as question particle (female/casual): 'What?' uses の to soften the question — feminine casual form. Unlike だい (male casual), かどうか (whether), かい (male yes/no), or か (formal).
ja-gram-n3-737-fill-1: の as female question marker: 'The shop is closed already?' — using の softens, implies curiosity not accusation. Different from かな (wonder), か (formal), だい (male), or かい (male).
ja-gram-n3-737-fill-2: の is female question form: 'Why don't you have a girlfriend?' uses の as softer alternative to か. Not だい (male), かどうか (whether), かい (male), or かな (I wonder).
ja-gram-n3-737-fill-3: の softens the apology: 'I have another engagement (you understand?)' — the の makes it matter-of-fact. Unlike かな (wonder), かしら (wonder-female), かどうか (whether), or か (formal).
ja-gram-n3-737-fill-4: の is female question particle: 'What's wrong?' uses の to soften questioning. Not か (formal), かい (male), かな (wonder), or かどうか (whether).
ja-gram-n3-893-fill-0: かどうか means 'whether or not' — uncertain conditions matter equally: 'It doesn't matter whether we know each other or not' — both cases same. Not だい (male), かな (wonder), の (female-casual), or か (simple).
ja-gram-n3-893-fill-1: かどうか shows 'if/whether': 'Do you know if Lucy can speak Japanese?' — uncertain condition to check. Different from かな (wonder), かしら (wonder-female), か (simple question), or の (female).
ja-gram-n3-893-fill-2: かどうか means 'whether': 'Do you know if he has a girlfriend?' — seeking knowledge of true/false. Not の (female), だい (male), かしら (wonder-female), or か (question).
ja-gram-n3-893-fill-3: かどうか introduces doubt: 'He asked after your health' — questioned whether you're well. Not かな (wonder), かい (male-casual), か (simple), or だい (male-casual).
ja-gram-n3-893-fill-4: かどうか tests condition: 'Try the shoes and see if they fit' — check the truth of fit. Not の (female), かな (wonder), だい (male), or かい (male).
ja-gram-n3-693-fill-0: か in 'whether' sense: 'Regardless of whether I wrote it or not' — か marks the question embedded. Different from かい (casual), のだ (explanation), だい (male), or 訳ではない (not saying).
ja-gram-n3-693-fill-1: か embeds a question: 'Don't you know who I am?' — か marks the embedded question. Not 訳ではない (not saying), かしら (wonder-female), の (female), or かどうか (whether).
ja-gram-n3-693-fill-2: か marks embedded question: 'I still don't know when it's going to be' — when is the embedded question. Not の (female), かい (casual), かどうか (whether), or 訳 (reason).
ja-gram-n3-693-fill-3: か embeds indirect question: 'Tell me who you went out with' — か marks who as questioned. Not かい (casual), かしら (wonder-female), だい (male), or 訳 (reason).
ja-gram-n3-693-fill-4: か forms embedded question: 'I asked where the documents are' — か marks location as unknown. Not 訳 (reason), 訳ではない (not saying), の (female), or かい (casual).
ja-gram-n3-749-fill-0: かな expresses wondering: 'I wonder if these shoes are expensive' — casual speculation. Not かどうか (whether), かい (casual-yes/no), か (simple), or の (female).
ja-gram-n3-749-fill-1: かな shows speculation: 'What would happen if I miss the deadline?' — pondering. Different from かしら (wonder-female), かい (casual), の (female), or か (simple).
ja-gram-n3-749-fill-2: かな expresses wondering: 'Is he seriously ill?' — casual uncertainty. Not か (simple), かどうか (whether), かい (casual), or かしら (wonder-female).
ja-gram-n3-749-fill-3: かな shows casual decision-like wondering: 'I guess I should go to a hospital' — thinking aloud. Not の (female), かしら (wonder-female), か (simple), or かい (casual).
ja-gram-n3-749-fill-4: かな wonders about timing: 'I wonder if early July is too early for the beach' — uncertain musing. Not かどうか (whether), かしら (wonder-female), かい (casual), or の (female).
ja-gram-n3-714-fill-0: だい is male casual question: 'Who is it?' — masculine casual form. Not の (female), かしら (wonder-female), かどうか (whether), or かな (wonder).
ja-gram-n3-714-fill-1: だい makes male casual question: 'What are you doing sneaking around?' — masculine form. Different from かな (wonder), か (formal), の (female), or かどうか (whether).
ja-gram-n3-714-fill-2: だい is male casual: 'Why are you still up?' — masculine questioning tone. Not の (female), かどうか (whether), かしら (wonder-female), or か (formal).
ja-gram-n3-714-fill-3: だい shows male casual questioning: 'What do you want to know?' — masculine form. Not かしら (wonder-female), か (formal), かな (wonder), or かどうか (whether).
ja-gram-n3-1774-fill-0: かしら is female wondering: 'I wonder who turned on the TV?' — feminine speculation. Not か (formal), かい (male-casual), だい (male), or かどうか (whether).
ja-gram-n3-1774-fill-1: かしら expresses female wondering: 'I wonder how much it is' — feminine musing. Different from かい (casual-male), か (formal), かな (wonder), or だい (male).
ja-gram-n3-1773-fill-0: かい is male casual yes/no question: 'Is it about to end?' — masculine casual. Not かな (wonder), かしら (wonder-female), か (formal), or かどうか (whether).
